reasoning: The baseline value for 2025-11-23 is derived from the latest available FRED data (2025-11-21, measuring 4.06). This 4.06 level is noticeably below the November average (typically hovering between 4.10 and 4.14) and recent consensus estimates from Reuters polls forecasting the 10-year yield to maintain around 4.10% over the coming months. Because 4.06 represents a recent local minimum, standard mean reversion strongly suggests a bounce above this figure in the very short term, supporting a higher probability of an increase by November 30 (0.65). Over longer horizons (December and February), financial assets behave increasingly like a random walk, leading to growing uncertainty and probabilities that regress toward 0.5, adhering to the standard forecasting principle that distant dates inherently feature wider variance and are less predictable. Therefore, probabilities scale down accordingly.
